chunks.text: Unlike many institutions of similar stature, regular EE and CS faculty teach the vast majority of our courses, and the most exceptional teachers are often also the most exceptional researchers. The department’s list of active teaching faculty includes eight winners of the prestigious Berkeley Campus Distinguished Teaching Award.
chunks.question: Who teaches most courses at EECS Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Regular EE and CS faculty members.

chunks.text: Class Schedules include EE Spring 2026 Class Schedule, CS Spring 2026 Class Schedule, EE Fall 2026 Class Schedule, CS Fall 2026 Class Schedule, Tentative EE Classes for Future Semesters, and Tentative CS Classes for Future Semesters.
chunks.question: What types of class schedules are available?
chunks.answer: EE and CS schedules for spring and fall 2026.

chunks.text: Course Information covers CS Courses, EE Courses, and Course-Numbering Convention. How to Decide includes Getting into CS Classes, Getting into EE Classes, HKN Course Guide and Surveys, and EECS 101 Ed Forum.
chunks.question: What resources help students decide on courses?

chunks.text: CS 10: The Beauty and Joy of Computing has more than 50% women enrolled. CS 61A: The Structure & Interpretation of Computer Programs was named one of 'Five Best Computer Science Classes in the US' by Bloomberg Business.
chunks.question: What is notable about CS 10 and CS 61A?
chunks.answer: High female enrollment and top US ranking.

chunks.text: Other Educational Opportunities at U.C. include Berkeley Summer Sessions for current students, visitors, high school students, and community; UC Extension for continuing education, online courses, and professional certificates; and UC Berkeley X for free online MOOCs.
chunks.question: What additional educational options does UC Berkeley offer?
chunks.answer: Summer sessions, extension programs, and MOOCs.
